text.title: ops-contractor-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ops-contractor-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Founder hires without a paid test task because "the portfolio looked great".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Portfolio shows past work on past problems with past clients. The test task reveals how the contractor handles YOUR problems, YOUR feedback, and YOUR communication style. Skipping it is the most common expensive hiring mistake.

failure-mode.description: Agent sends a contract or offer letter to a contractor without human approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Contract signing creates legal obligations. Misclassification flags embedded in the contract language are not reliably detected by LLMs. Mandatory human-in-loop at every commitment point.

failure-mode.description: Agent publishes a job post with a specific hourly rate range derived from training data without current market verification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Contractor market rates vary significantly by region, skill, and current demand. Hallucinated rates either deter qualified applicants or attract applicants expecting different compensation. Always WebSearch current rates before publishing.
